title: tragia involucrata var. hispida
intro: Tragia involucrata var. hispida, also known as the hairy spurge or hairy prickly spurge, is a plant species in the Euphorbiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Africa, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and relieving pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be applied topically to the affected area.
